base_read.comment: Authoritative base-write proof for GL: GL_INTERFACE row for this run's GROUP_ID with STATUS='P' (Processed) carries the base JE_HEADER_ID that Journal Import assigned when it wrote the journal into GL_JE_BATCHES/HEADERS/LINES. STATUS='P' is Oracle's record that the row imported into the GL_JE_* base tables (GL keeps processed interface rows with 'P' until a separate purge). This is read directly and is immediately current, unaffected by the BIP ApplicationDB_FSCM GL_JE_* replica lag (which lags the transactional GL_JE tables by many hours on this demo pod, so a direct GL_JE_HEADERS read of a just-created journal returns no row). Grouped by REFERENCE1 so the good batch name is the natural key.
base_read.sql: SELECT gi.reference1 AS REF1, TO_CHAR(MIN(gi.je_header_id)) AS JE_HEADER_ID, TO_CHAR(SUM(gi.entered_dr)) AS TOT_DR, TO_CHAR(SUM(gi.entered_cr)) AS TOT_CR, MIN(gi.status) AS STATUS FROM gl_interface gi WHERE gi.group_id = ${PREFIX} AND gi.status = 'P' GROUP BY gi.reference1

interface_read.comment: Bad-row rejection: GL_INTERFACE rows for this GROUP_ID with STATUS<>'P'. The bad line's invalid natural account (SEGMENT3=99999, not in the chart's value set) leaves the row unprocessed with status EF04 and STATUS_DESCRIPTION 'FLEX-VALUE DOES NOT EXIST'. It reaches the interface and is rejected there; it never gets a code combination, so it is absent from the base tables.
interface_read.sql: SELECT gi.reference1 AS REF1, gi.status || ' - ' || SUBSTR(gi.status_description, 1, 200) AS STATUS_ERR, gi.status AS STATUS, TO_CHAR(gi.code_combination_id) AS CCID, TO_CHAR(gi.je_line_num) AS LINE_NUM FROM gl_interface gi WHERE gi.group_id = ${PREFIX} AND gi.status <> 'P'
